message: county level maps for CMS, NCHS; fix standardization of age, FIPS for these files
add new data files and update JSON for CMS disparities dashboard

cms_acute_myocardial_infarction.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of acute_myocardial_infarction
cms_acute_myocardial_infarction.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of acute_myocardial_infarction.
cms_acute_myocardial_infarction.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of acute_myocardial_infarction. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
cms_acute_myocardial_infarction.statement: The viral activity level of acute_myocardial_infarction in wastewater is {value}.

cms_opioid_use_disorder_overarching.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder
cms_opioid_use_disorder_overarching.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder.
cms_opioid_use_disorder_overarching.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
cms_opioid_use_disorder_overarching.statement: The viral activity level of Overarching Opioid use disorder in wastewater is {value}.

cms_mmd.name: Mapping Medicare Disparities by Population Tool
cms_mmd.organization: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS)
cms_mmd.description: The Mapping Medicare Disparities (MMD) by Population Tool is an interactive map that displays chronic disease prevalence, costs, hospitalization, and preventive care utilization data for Medicare Fee-for-Service beneficiaries. Data are available at the national, state, and county levels, stratified by age, race/ethnicity, and sex. Condition prevalence rates are calculated using ICD-10 diagnosis codes in Medicare claims data, following the Chronic Conditions Warehouse (CCW) definitions. The tool covers over 30 chronic conditions including diabetes, hypertension, COPD, heart failure, and mental health conditions, as well as preventive service utilization metrics. Data are updated annually.
cms_mmd.restrictions: Public domain. CMS data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.
